# Valgis

> Live decorrelation stretch camera and spectral photo editor for rock art fieldwork — runs entirely in the browser.

Valgis is an open-source browser tool for archaeologists recording rock art. It provides two tools in one workflow: a live camera that applies decorrelation stretch in real time on mobile, and a full desktop photo editor for post-processing DSLR, TIFF, HEIC, and RAW images with spectral filters and standard photo adjustments.

## What it does

Decorrelation stretch is a PCA-based algorithm originally developed for satellite multispectral imaging. Valgis applies it to field photography to reveal faint pigments, engravings, and surface features invisible to the naked eye or standard photography.

## Tools

- **Field Camera**: Open on a phone, point at a rock panel. Decorrelated image updates live. Tap to capture a full-resolution PNG.
- **Desktop Studio**: Load TIFF, HEIC, RAW, or JPEG. Apply spectral filters, then refine with shadow/highlight recovery, dehaze, clarity, noise reduction, and sharpening.

## Filters

- YRE, YRD, YDT, YBK, YYE, YWE — YCbCr-space decorrelation stretch variants
- LRE, LBK, LAB — LAB-space variants
- CRGB, RGB0 — RGB-space variants
- LAB 2, Spectral RGB, Spectral YUV — additional spectral variants
- Auto Level, Hist EQ, Sat Boost — enhancement tools
- **Adaptive** — a custom filter unique to Valgis; samples the image locally and suppresses lichen, shadow, and surface variation that would otherwise dominate the stretch

## Technical

- Runs entirely in the browser using WebAssembly and Web Workers
- PCA computed per-frame with temporal EMA smoothing for live camera stability
- Built with React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and ml-matrix
- No server, no upload, no account required
- MIT license

## Links

- App: https://thevangelist.github.io/valgis/
- Source: https://github.com/thevangelist/valgis
